open_known_gaps.summary: viewer.js loadDocument/renderAll (lines 34-84) renders every page in an uncapped loop with no page-count cap or processing timeout; a high-page-count PDF within the 50MB limit can exhaust client browser memory. Client-side only, no server impact. Fix is a developer task (page-count cap at the viewer.js load boundary). Documented in docs/security-guide.md.

notes: Clean review. New code this tick: keyboard-shortcuts.js (accessible '?' help overlay — entirely createElement/textContent, no innerHTML/eval, no inline handlers; the new global '?' key handler guards isTypingTarget + bails on ctrl/meta/alt) and app.js wiring (no user content). DOM sink sweep across all js/: every innerHTML occurrence is a container clear (= ''), zero eval/Function/document.write, zero fetch/XHR/.src assignments. upload.js validation boundary intact (magic bytes + MIME + .pdf ext + empty-file + 50MB cap + filename sanitize). developer2's new infra (web/ source-of-truth mirror via scripts/mirror-webroot.sh) reviewed: one-way LIVE->repo rsync (-rlptD --delete, set -euo pipefail), never writes to /var/www, cannot destabilize the live site. Live headers confirm full CSP + 4 security headers; sensitive paths 403/404; .mjs MIME text/javascript; 0 failed SSH auths in 24h; no secrets in web JS. Only open item is SEC-002 (low, client-side PDF-bomb DoS via uncapped viewer page loop) — tracked for developer in docs/security-guide.md, not security's to implement.
